Title: Pyruvate transporter BnaBASS2 impacts seed oil accumulation in Brassica napus

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bile acid:sodium symporter family protein 2 (BASS2); sodium-dependent pyruvate transporter; predicted membrane protein with nine transmembrane regions
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; nutritional oil trait
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing and mature seeds; oil bodies
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased seed oil content and TAG accumulation, whereas knockout reduced seed oil content, TAG accumulation, seed weight, and yield-related traits
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in B. napus; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; subcellular localization with GFP; qRT-PCR; Western blot; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; lipidomics; GC-FID fatty acid/oil quantification; TEM observation of oil bodies; agronomic trait analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in multiple tissues, especially developing siliques and seeds
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in multiple tissues, especially developing siliques and seeds
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; developing seeds sampled mainly at 31 DAF; field and growth-chamber evaluations
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; 2×35S promoter for GFP localization construct
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; lipidomics by LC-MS; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of BnaA05.BASS2 together with BnaC04.BASS2-1 reduced silique number per plant, seed number per silique, thousand seed weight, yield per plant, and increased seed protein/soluble sugar while reducing starch. Overexpression decreased seed protein and soluble sugar but showed no obvious negative effect on most agronomic traits
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve seed oil content in rapeseed by overexpression or activation, with limited agronomic penalty in OE lines; caution that knockout causes yield penalty
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions as a plastid pyruvate importer affecting glycolysis-, fatty acid synthesis-, and energy-related metabolites in seed chloroplasts. It is not a catalytic pathway enzyme or TF, but a transporter controlling precursor supply to plastid fatty acid synthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA05.BASS2 encodes a chloroplast envelope-localized sodium-dependent pyruvate transporter highly expressed in developing siliques and seeds. 2. Overexpression increased chloroplast glycolytic intermediates, acetyl-CoA, ATP/NAD(P)H status, TAG accumulation, oil body enlargement, and mature seed oil content. 3. CRISPR disruption together with BnaC04.BASS2-1 decreased these metabolites, reduced seed oil content by about 2.8%-5.0%, and caused yield-related penalties.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bile acid:sodium symporter family protein 2 (BASS2); sodium-dependent pyruvate transporter; predicted membrane protein with nine transmembrane regions
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; nutritional oil trait
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing and mature seeds; oil bodies
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Joint disruption with BnaA05.BASS2 reduced seed oil content and TAG accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 double-mutant analysis with BnaA05.BASS2; sequencing; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; lipidomics; GC-FID oil quantification; agronomic trait analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in multiple tissues, especially developing siliques and seeds
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in multiple tissues, especially developing siliques and seeds
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; developing seeds sampled mainly at 31 DAF
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; lipidomics by LC-MS; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss in double mutants reduced silique number per plant, seed number per silique, thousand seed weight, and yield per plant
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful homolog to preserve or activate in breeding for high oil content; avoid disruptive edits
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied functionally as part of double mutants with BnaA05.BASS2 rather than individually
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC04.BASS2-1 is a highly expressed BASS2 paralog in developing siliques and seeds. 2. CRISPR mutation together with BnaA05.BASS2 caused reduced plastid glycolysis/fatty acid metabolites, lower TAG, and lower seed oil content. 3. The gene acts redundantly with BnaA05.BASS2 in promoting rapeseed seed oil accumulation.
